encoder.c(3) Library Functions Manual encoder.c(3)

NAME

encoder.c - FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct h265_encoder

Typedefs


typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Functions


h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)
void h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t *ascii_data, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

Definition in file encoder.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t * ascii_data, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Encode an ASCII art frame

The encoder automatically detects size changes and reinitializes when needed. The output packet includes frame dimensions so decoder can reconfigure.

Parameters

encoder Encoder handle
width Current frame width (characters)
height Current frame height (characters)
ascii_data Raw ASCII character grid (width × height bytes)
output_buf Buffer for encoded output
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

OUTPUT PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...]

Definition at line 291 of file encoder.c.

292                                                                         {
293   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_1] Starting h265_encode %ux%u", width, height);
294   if (!encoder || !ascii_data || !output_buf || !output_size) {
295     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder arguments");
296   }
297 
298   if (*output_size < 5) {
299     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
300   }
301 
302   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_2] Reconfigure %ux%u -> %ux%u", encoder->current_width, encoder->current_height, width, height);
303   asciichat_error_t result = h265_encoder_reconfigure(encoder, width, height);
304   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_3] Reconfigure returned %d", result);
305   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
306     return result;
307   }
308 
309   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_4] Converting ASCII to YUV420");
310   h265_encoder_ascii_to_yuv420(ascii_data, width, height, encoder->yuv_buf);
311   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_5] YUV420 conversion done");
312 
313   // Setup frame data pointers
314   encoder->frame->data[0] = encoder->yuv_buf;
315   encoder->frame->data[1] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height;
316   encoder->frame->data[2] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height + (width / 2) * (height / 2);
317   encoder->frame->linesize[0] = width;
318   encoder->frame->linesize[1] = width / 2;
319   encoder->frame->linesize[2] = width / 2;
320   encoder->frame->width = width;
321   encoder->frame->height = height;
322   encoder->frame->format = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
323 
324   // Request keyframe if needed
325   if (encoder->request_keyframe) {
326     encoder->frame->pict_type = AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I;
327     encoder->request_keyframe = false;
328   }
329 
330   // Encode frame
331   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_6] Sending frame to encoder: %ux%u format=%d", width, height, encoder->frame->format);
332   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->frame);
333   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_7] avcodec_send_frame returned %d", send_ret);
334   if (send_ret < 0) {
335     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_send_frame failed: %d", send_ret);
336     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to send frame to encoder");
337   }
338 
339   log_info("[H265_ENCODE_8] Receiving encoded packet...");
340   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
341   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
342     // EAGAIN is normal - encoder needs more frames before outputting a packet
343     log_dev("H265_ENCODE: EAGAIN - encoder buffering frames (normal), waiting for next frame");
344     *output_size = 0; // No output this time
345     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
346   }
347   if (recv_ret < 0) {
348     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d (EAGAIN=%d)", recv_ret, AVERROR(EAGAIN));
349     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive encoded packet");
350   }
351   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Got packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
352 
353   uint8_t flags = 0;
354   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
355     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
356     encoder->keyframes++;
357   }
358 
359   if (width != encoder->current_width || height != encoder->current_height) {
360     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE;
361   }
362 
363   size_t header_size = 5;
364   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
365   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
366 
367   if (required_size > *output_size) {
368     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
369   }
370 
371   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
372   output_buf[0] = flags;
373   output_buf[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
374   output_buf[2] = width & 0xFF;
375   output_buf[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
376   output_buf[4] = height & 0xFF;
377 
378   // Write encoded data
379   if (packet_size > 0) {
380     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
381   }
382 
383   *output_size = required_size;
384   encoder->total_frames++;
385   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_DONE] Encoding complete, output_size=%zu", required_size);
386 
387   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
388 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE,
    log_debug, log_dev, log_error, log_info, and
    SET_ERRNO.






h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t
  initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)


Create a new x265 encoder for ASCII frames




Parameters


initial_width Initial frame width (characters)


initial_height Initial frame height (characters)


Returns


Encoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 54 of file encoder.c.


54                                                                                      {
55   if (initial_width == 0 || initial_height == 0) {
56     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
57     return NULL;
58   }
59 
60   h265_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_encoder_t), h265_encoder_t *);
61   if (!enc) {
62     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate encoder structure");
63     return NULL;
64   }
65 
66   // Find best available HEVC encoder (try by ID first, then by name for specific implementations)
67   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
68 
69   // If generic HEVC encoder not found, try specific implementations by name
70   if (!codec) {
71     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("libx265");
72   }
73   if (!codec) {
74     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_vaapi");
75   }
76   if (!codec) {
77     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_videotoolbox");
78   }
79   if (!codec) {
80     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_nvenc");
81   }
82   if (!codec) {
83     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_qsv");
84   }
85 
86   if (!codec) {
87     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC encoder not found");
88     SAFE_FREE(enc);
89     return NULL;
90   }
91 
92   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
93   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
94     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
95     SAFE_FREE(enc);
96     return NULL;
97   }
98 
99   // Configure codec context
100   enc->codec_ctx->width = initial_width;
101   enc->codec_ctx->height = initial_height;
102   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
103   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, 30};
104   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){30, 1};
105 
106   // Set ultrafast preset for low-latency encoding
107   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "preset", "ultrafast", 0) < 0) {
108     log_warn("Failed to set preset to ultrafast, using default");
109   }
110   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "tune", "zerolatency", 0) < 0) {
111     log_warn("Failed to set tune to zerolatency, using default");
112   }
113 
114   enc->codec_ctx->thread_count = h265_thread_count();
115 
116   // Defer avcodec_open2 to first encode call. The initial resolution (800x600) is
117   // usually wrong — the actual frame resolution is only known when the first capture
118   // arrives. Opening the codec here would create an x265 thread pool (~1s) that gets
119   // immediately discarded on reconfigure, wasting startup time.
120   enc->codec_opened = false;
121 
122   // Allocate frame and packet
123   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
124   enc->packet = av_packet_alloc();
125 
126   if (!enc->frame || !enc->packet) {
127     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
128     if (enc->frame)
129       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
130     if (enc->packet)
131       av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
132     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
133     SAFE_FREE(enc);
134     return NULL;
135   }
136 
137   enc->current_width = initial_width;
138   enc->current_height = initial_height;
139 
140   // Allocate YUV420P buffer (Y plane + U/V planes)
141   size_t stride = initial_width;
142   enc->yuv_buf_size = stride * initial_height * 3 / 2;
143   enc->yuv_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(enc->yuv_buf_size, uint8_t *);
144   if (!enc->yuv_buf) {
145     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate YUV buffer");
146     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
147     av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
148     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
149     SAFE_FREE(enc);
150     return NULL;
151   }
152 
153   log_info("FFmpeg HEVC encoder created: %ux%u (codec: %s)", initial_width, initial_height, codec->name);
154 
155   // Register encoder for debugging
156   NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER(enc, "h265_encoder", NULL);
157 
158   return enc;
159 }






References ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Destroy an x265 encoder




Parameters


encoder Encoder to destroy (may be NULL)


Definition at line 161 of file encoder.c.


161                                                    {
162   if (!encoder)
163     return;
164 
165   if (encoder->frame) {
166     av_frame_free(&encoder->frame);
167   }
168   if (encoder->packet) {
169     av_packet_free(&encoder->packet);
170   }
171   if (encoder->codec_ctx) {
172     // Capture x265 encoder cleanup output (final frame stats, etc.)
173     LOG_IO("hevc", {
174       avcodec_free_context(&encoder->codec_ctx);
175     });
176   }
177   if (encoder->yuv_buf) {
178     SAFE_FREE(encoder->yuv_buf);
179   }
180   SAFE_FREE(encoder);
181 }






References LOG_IO, and SAFE_FREE.






asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush
  (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t *
  output_size)


Flush remaining buffered frames from encoder




Sends a NULL frame to the encoder to flush all buffered frames.
    This should be called before destroying the encoder or when you need to
    ensure all encoded data is retrieved.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


output_buf Buffer for encoded output


output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ASCIICHAT_OK with
  size=0 when no more data)


USAGE: // After final h265_encode() call, flush remaining
    frames while (size > 0) { size = max_size; h265_encoder_flush(encoder,
    buf, &size); if (size > 0) { // Send the flushed frame... } }


Definition at line 396 of file encoder.c.


396                                                                                                         {
397   if (!encoder || !output_buf || !output_size) {
398     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder or output parameters for flush");
399   }
400 
401   if (*output_size < 5) {
402     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
403   }
404 
405   // Send NULL frame to signal end of stream (flushes encoder)
406   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Sending NULL frame to encoder to flush buffered data");
407   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, NULL);
408   if (send_ret < 0) {
409     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_send_frame(NULL) failed: %d", send_ret);
410     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to flush encoder");
411   }
412 
413   // Try to receive a packet
414   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Attempting to receive flushed packet...");
415   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
416   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
417     log_dev("H265_FLUSH: EAGAIN - no more frames available");
418     *output_size = 0;
419     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
420   }
421   if (recv_ret == AVERROR_EOF) {
422     log_debug("H265_FLUSH: EOF - encoder is flushed");
423     *output_size = 0;
424     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
425   }
426   if (recv_ret < 0) {
427     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d", recv_ret);
428     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive flushed packet");
429   }
430 
431   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Got flushed packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
432 
433   // Check if packet size exceeds output buffer
434   size_t header_size = 5;
435   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
436   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
437 
438   if (required_size > *output_size) {
439     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
440   }
441 
442   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
443   uint8_t flags = 0;
444   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
445     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
446     encoder->keyframes++;
447   }
448 
449   output_buf[0] = flags;
450   output_buf[1] = (encoder->current_width >> 8) & 0xFF;
451   output_buf[2] = encoder->current_width & 0xFF;
452   output_buf[3] = (encoder->current_height >> 8) & 0xFF;
453   output_buf[4] = encoder->current_height & 0xFF;
454 
455   // Write encoded data
456   if (packet_size > 0) {
457     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
458   }
459 
460   *output_size = required_size;
461   encoder->total_frames++;
462 
463   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
464 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, log_debug, log_dev,
    log_error, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint64_t *
  total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint32_t *
  avg_bitrate)


Get encoder statistics




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


total_frames Output: total frames encoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes encoded


avg_bitrate Output: average bitrate in bits per second


Definition at line 466 of file encoder.c.


467                                                    {
468   if (!encoder)
469     return;
470 
471   if (total_frames)
472     *total_frames = encoder->total_frames;
473   if (keyframes)
474     *keyframes = encoder->keyframes;
475   if (avg_bitrate)
476     *avg_bitrate = encoder->avg_bitrate;
477 }






Referenced by h265_client_get_stats().






void
  h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Request a keyframe on the next encode




Forces the encoder to produce an I-frame (keyframe) on the next
    call to h265_encoder_encode(). Useful for error recovery or stream
    synchronization.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


Definition at line 390 of file encoder.c.


390                                                             {
391   if (encoder) {
392     encoder->request_keyframe = true;
393   }
394 }
